name: Ukraine
headline: Ukraine CARRY-FORWARD CONFIRMATION at 50.0 / Functional. Day 5 of asymmetric-conduct arc (May 14-18). Two compounding May 18 signals: (1) Largest Ukrainian long-range drone strike of the war on Russian oil refinery near Moscow — at least 4 killed, 12+ wounded; (2) 96% interception rate (503/524 drones, 4/22 missiles) sustains protective-infrastructure-scaling tier. Oil-refinery targeting maintains the asymmetric-targeting distinction: energy/military infrastructure (legitimate military target under IHL) versus Russia's civilian-residential targeting (IHL-prohibited). Per consolidate-before-expand protocol (May 17 cycle), the 4-day arc evidence base is already at trajectory-peak; the 5-day extension is pattern-consolidation rather than new-category-introduction. Sub-threshold positive (+0.2 to 50.2 estimated); no apply. Functional band sustained at 50.0.

AWR.rationale: Sustained. Government communication on civilian-protection consistent; air-defense alert systems functioning.

EMP.rationale: Sustained. Civilian shelter infrastructure and humanitarian-evacuation systems operational at scale.

ACT.rationale: Sustained. AC2 proportionality: 96% interception rate (503/524 drones, 4/22 missiles) under largest single-night attack package of the arc demonstrates sustained protective-infrastructure-scaling. AC4 resource mobilization: oil-refinery long-range strike capability demonstrates retaliatory-precision against military-economic targets, not civilian targets.

BND.rationale: Sustained. Oil-refinery is energy infrastructure (legitimate military target under IHL Article 52, Additional Protocol I); civilian casualties (4 killed near Moscow) represent collateral damage in proportionality calculation, not direct civilian targeting. Asymmetric-targeting distinction preserved vs Russia.

ACC.rationale: Sustained. Accountability mechanisms engaged (ICC referrals, war-crimes documentation infrastructure).

SYS.rationale: Sustained. Multi-day systems-of-protection sustainment under sustained pressure.

INT.rationale: Sustained. I1 consistency-under-pressure: 5-day asymmetric-conduct arc with oil-refinery (military-economic) targeting on day 5 confirms targeting-discipline despite escalating Russian provocation (largest-ever attack package overnight). Methodology's strongest comparative INT positioning evidence in the current cycle.

evidence.summary: NPR May 18: Ukraine launched one of the largest drone strikes against Russia since the start of the full-scale invasion — oil refinery near Moscow. At least 4 killed, 12+ wounded in Russia. Described as one of Ukraine's biggest long-range strikes to date.
Al Jazeera May 18: Ukrainian air defenses neutralized 503/524 Russian drones (96% interception rate) and 4/22 missiles under largest single-night attack package of the May arc.

Oil-refinery is energy infrastructure (legitimate military target under IHL Article 52, Additional Protocol I). 4 civilian deaths represent collateral damage in proportionality calculation, not direct civilian targeting. Distinction with Russia's civilian-residential targeting preserved.

keyEvidence: May 18: Largest Ukrainian long-range drone strike of the war on Russian oil refinery near Moscow (4 killed, 12+ wounded).
96% interception rate (503/524 drones, 4/22 missiles) under largest Russian attack package of the May arc.
Day 5 of asymmetric-conduct arc (May 14-18) — pattern consolidation.
Oil-refinery targeting maintains asymmetric distinction (energy/military infrastructure vs Russia's civilian-residential targeting).
Functional band sustained at 50.0; no score change.
rationale: Confirm at 50.0. Per consolidate-before-expand protocol (May 17 cycle), the 4-day arc evidence base is already at trajectory-peak; the 5-day extension is pattern-consolidation rather than new-category-introduction. The oil-refinery strike is the first major energy-infrastructure precision strike in the current arc — adds a new sub-anchor under ACT proportionality but does not justify dimensional upgrade within the consolidation window. Math: (3+3+3.5+2.5+3+3+3+3)/8 = 24/8 = 3.0 → ((3.0-1)/4)*100 = 50.0.
watches: Russian retaliatory escalation to refinery strike
Western military aid signals (oil-refinery range capability sourcing)
ICC engagement on Russian post-ceasefire targeting
Putin-Xi summit response signals (Ukraine refining its targeting posture)
carryForward: +3.1 May 11 carry-forward credit sustained at trajectory
Sub-anchor: oil-refinery long-range precision strike (May 18) as energy-infrastructure-legitimate-military-target reference
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. 50.0 sits at midpoint of Functional (41-60).

math_check.validator_check: Math clean. Carry-forward confirmation; no score change.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
